biologyPathway.detail: Inflammation can make the voice hoarse, weak, rough, or absent.

biologyPathway.title: Common Causes
biologyPathway.detail: Acute cases are often viral or strain-related; chronic cases can involve reflux, irritants, overuse, lesions, or other disease.

biologyPathway.title: Voice Rest

clinical.label: Strictly Medical
clinical.title: Laryngeal Inflammation & Voice-Strain Cascade

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Inflamed Voice Box
body: Laryngitis inflames the larynx and vocal cords.
Hoarseness is the speaker distortion, not an invitation to keep yelling through it.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Causes
body: Viral infection, vocal strain, reflux, smoking, irritants, allergies, and chronic sinus drainage can contribute.
Chronic hoarseness may need ENT evaluation.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Care
body: Voice rest, hydration, humidification, reflux care, and avoiding irritants may help depending on cause.
Whispering can still strain the voice. Humanity even found a way to rest incorrectly.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Safety Check
body: Seek urgent care for breathing trouble, coughing blood, severe pain, neck swelling, or trouble swallowing.
Hoarseness lasting more than a few weeks needs evaluation.

paragraphs: Your voice is a speaker that has been overdriven until the signal turned scratchy.
The amplifier, your larynx, is inflamed and deeply unimpressed.
Maybe it is a virus. Maybe shouting. Maybe reflux, smoke, allergies, or chronic irritation.
Turn down the volume, hydrate, rest the voice, and get checked if it lingers.
Repair the speaker or keep shouting through a blown amplifier. Your choice.

steps: Sit upright and relax the throat/jaw.
Place one hand on the throat and one on the heart.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds.
Exhale silently for 8 seconds.
Think: My voice matters. I protect the instrument.

Symbolic interpretation never replaces diagnosis or treatment.

biologyPathway.short: The clear surface tissue gets irritated.
biologyPathway.detail: The conjunctiva lines the eyelid and covers the white of the eye.

biologyPathway.title: Types
biologyPathway.detail: Watery discharge often fits viral/allergic patterns; thick discharge may suggest bacterial, but diagnosis is still context-dependent.

biologyPathway.title: Contagion
biologyPathway.detail: Hand hygiene, no towel sharing, and avoiding eye touching matter. A stunning blow to humanity's face-touching career.

clinical.label: Strictly Medical

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Red Eye Is a Category
body: Conjunctivitis means inflammation of the conjunctiva.
Red eye can also come from conditions more serious than standard pink eye.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Cause Matters
body: Viral, bacterial, allergic, irritant, contact-lens-related, and other causes need different handling.
Antibiotics are not confetti. They need a reason.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Hygiene
body: Wash hands, avoid touching eyes, avoid sharing towels, and replace/avoid contaminated eye makeup or contacts as advised.
The eye is not a community project.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Safety Check
body: Eye pain, vision change, light sensitivity, contact lens use, trauma, chemical exposure, severe redness, herpes concern, or newborn symptoms needs urgent eye care.
Do not wait around with a painful red eye.

Maybe it is viral. Maybe bacterial. Maybe allergies. Maybe irritant. Maybe contact lenses are staging a coup.
Clean hands, stop touching it, avoid sharing towels, and get care if pain or vision changes show up.
Rinse the goggles properly or keep staring through sandpaper like that was a plan. Your choice.

Do not press on the eye.

biologyPathway.title: Mucus Drainage
biologyPathway.detail: Excess or thick mucus can drain from the nasal passages into the throat, causing cough, throat clearing, hoarseness, or a lump sensation.

biologyPathway.title: Many Causes
biologyPathway.short: Allergy, infection, reflux, irritants, meds, structure.
biologyPathway.detail: The same drip can come from very different causes, because the human airway apparently needed a mystery mechanic.

biologyPathway.title: Cause-Based Treatment
biologyPathway.detail: Allergy care, nasal sprays, sinus treatment, reflux care, hydration, humidification, or irritant avoidance may help depending on cause.

biologyPathway.title: When to Check
biologyPathway.short: Persistent or severe symptoms need care.
biologyPathway.detail: Fever, bloody discharge, severe pain, chronic symptoms, or breathing/swallowing trouble deserves evaluation.

clinical.label: Strictly Medical
clinical.title: Mucus Drainage, Throat Irritation & Cause Search

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Symptom, Not Diagnosis
body: Post-nasal drip means mucus is draining into the back of the throat.
It can cause cough, throat clearing, hoarseness, sore throat, and a constant need to swallow.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Cause List
body: Causes include allergies, sinusitis, viral infection, GERD/reflux, smoke, pollution, dry air, medications, hormones, pregnancy, deviated septum, and nasal polyps.
Same drip, many plumbing disasters.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Treatment Direction
body: Treatment depends on the cause and may include allergy treatment, nasal steroid/saline, reflux care, hydration, humidifier, irritant avoidance, or ENT evaluation.
Do not keep mashing the allergy button if reflux is kicking the back door.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Safety Check
body: Seek care for fever, severe facial pain, bloody discharge, trouble breathing/swallowing, persistent symptoms, recurrent sinus infections, or concerning hoarseness.
The faucet gets diagnosed before you blame your entire personality.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Leaky Faucet
paragraphs: Your nasal passages are a plumbing system with a faucet that will not stop dripping.
The sink is your throat. The drip is mucus. The homeowner keeps clearing the throat like that will fix the pipe.
Maybe it is allergies. Maybe reflux. Maybe sinus infection. Maybe dry air. Maybe smoke. Maybe medication. Very cool, very normal, very annoying.
Fix the cause, not just the sound effect.
Fix the faucet properly or keep clearing your throat like the plumbing union abandoned you. Your choice.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Clear It Properly Reality Check
paragraphs: You may have post-nasal drip. You may also have a holding-and-clearing pattern.
Medical first: post-nasal drip is a symptom. Allergies, reflux, sinus disease, irritants, meds, and structural issues do not use the same fix.
Symbolically, ask what you keep swallowing instead of expressing.
But physically, track triggers and treat the actual cause.
Fix the faucet properly or keep clearing your throat like the plumbing union abandoned you. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Lung-Spleen Dampness
paragraphs: In TCM imagery, dampness can symbolize mucus, heaviness, and unprocessed grief/worry.
Medically, post-nasal drip still needs cause-based care.

influenceLayers.title: Unreleased Self

The Releaser clears the throat without ignoring sinus/reflux reality.

influenceLayers.title: Leaky Faucet

The goal is fixing the valve, not yelling at the sink.

This layer asks what is being held there.

influenceLayers.title: Clear the Line
paragraphs: The reset is hydration, trigger tracking, and cause-based care.
Less throat clearing as a hobby.

reset.title: Faucet Repair & Throat-Clearing Reset
steps: Sit upright with one hand on the throat and one on the chest.

clinical.label: Strictly Medical

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Seizure Is Brain Electrical Disturbance
body: A seizure is a sudden uncontrolled electrical disturbance in the brain.
It may involve convulsions, staring, altered awareness, strange sensations, automatisms, emotional changes, or collapse.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Causes and Triggers
body: Causes can include epilepsy, fever in children, low blood sugar, electrolyte imbalance, infection, head trauma, stroke, tumor, medication/substance effects, withdrawal, sleep deprivation, and toxins.
Stress and poor sleep can lower seizure threshold in susceptible people, but they are not the whole story.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — First Aid
body: Keep the person safe, move hazards away, cushion the head, time the seizure, turn them on their side when possible, and stay until recovery.
Do not restrain them and do not put anything in their mouth. The mouth myth needs to be launched into the sun.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Emergency and Follow-Up
body: Call emergency services if it is a first seizure, lasts over 5 minutes, repeats without recovery, happens in water, causes injury, involves pregnancy/diabetes, causes breathing trouble, or recovery does not happen normally.
Known epilepsy needs neurologic management, medication adherence, trigger planning, and safety precautions.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty

paragraphs: Your brain is a power plant and a lightning storm just hit the control room.
The circuits surge, the lights flicker, and the maintenance crew needs a safety plan instead of someone yelling calm down at electricity.
First aid is boring but heroic: protect the head, clear hazards, time it, side position when possible, no mouth objects, no restraining.
Then comes medical evaluation, trigger review, neurology, medications when needed, and sleep like a civilized mammal if possible.
Ground the power plant medically or keep pretending lightning is a lifestyle choice. Your choice.

Afterward, rest safely and arrange medical/neurology follow-up if needed.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Stuttering Is Real and Involuntary
body: Stuttering involves disruptions in speech fluency such as repetitions, prolongations, and blocks.
It is not laziness, weakness, stupidity, or someone failing to try hard enough. Apparently this still needs saying because humans enjoy being awful in groups.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Causes and Contributors
body: Developmental stuttering often begins in childhood and has neurologic/genetic contributors.
Stress, anxiety, pressure, time demands, and shame can worsen stuttering but should not be framed as the sole cause.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Sudden-Onset Safety
body: Sudden adult-onset stuttering can occur with stroke, head injury, seizure, medication/substance effects, or neurologic disease.
If speech change comes with weakness, numbness, facial droop, confusion, vision changes, severe headache, seizure, or head injury, get urgent care.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Treatment and Support

The goal is communication, not forcing the person to perform fluency for everyone else's comfort.

witty.metaphorText: Your immune system is a security guard who cannot tell pollen from a bank robber.
witty.wittyAdvice: Retrain the security guard safely or keep letting pollen get treated like an armed intruder. Your choice.

brutal.realityCheck: You may have allergies. You may also have a hypervigilance-and-boundary pattern.

critical_alerts: Trouble breathing, wheezing, throat/tongue/lip swelling, or tight throat
Dizziness, fainting, confusion, low blood pressure symptoms, or collapse
Widespread hives plus vomiting, breathing symptoms, swelling, or rapid worsening
Known severe allergy exposure, insect sting allergy, food allergy reaction, or need for epinephrine
Asthma symptoms during allergic reaction
seek_immediate_care_if: Anaphylaxis is suspected, breathing/swelling symptoms occur, dizziness/fainting occurs, or reaction rapidly worsens
schedule_care_if: Allergy symptoms are recurrent, severe, unclear, trigger-limited life, asthma-related, or require testing/immunotherapy planning
medical_safety.self_care_allowed_if: Trigger avoidance, saline rinse, allergen reduction, and appropriate OTC allergy meds may help mild known allergies without red flags.
medical_safety.do_not_ignore: Allergies can become life-threatening when airway, circulation, or multiple body systems are involved.
medical_safety.disclaimer: This app is for education and self-reflection only. It does not diagnose, treat, prevent, or cure allergies.

critical_alerts: Sudden, severe, or excruciating pain with no obvious cause
Pain that begins after a major physical injury or trauma
Pain accompanied by fever, chills, night sweats, or unexplained weight loss
Pain with new neurological deficits like weakness, numbness, or loss of coordination
Pain that steadily worsens despite rest, splinting, or gentle care
New pain in a patient with a personal history of cancer
seek_immediate_care_if: You experience sudden, unbearable pain or pain following a major injury
Your pain is accompanied by high fever, confusion, or a cold, pale limb
You experience sudden weakness, numbness, or loss of bowel or bladder control
schedule_care_if: Your pain is persistent, gradually worsening, or lasts longer than 2-3 weeks
You have pain with unexplained weight loss, night sweats, or a history of cancer
Your joint or muscle pain is accompanied by persistent morning stiffness or visible swelling
medical_safety.self_care_allowed_if: Gentle movement, thermal therapy (ice/heat), and rest are reasonable for managing mild, transient muscular strains or familiar joint stiffness.
medical_safety.do_not_ignore: Chronic or severe pain should not be masked with analgesics without a professional diagnosis of the underlying physical cause.
medical_safety.disclaimer: This app is for education and self-reflection only. It does not diagnose, treat, prevent, or cure disease. Seek professional medical care for diagnosis, treatment, medication decisions, or urgent symptoms.
